title: Clusterin Phospholipid Particles Flotation Assay Using an Optiprep Step Gradient
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"em0yq","text":"This protocol is based on Yeh FL et al. Neuron (2016) and details how to efficiently separate Clusterin phospholipid particles (Clusterin:DMPC) from free Clusterin together with binders by flotation using an Optiprep step density gradient.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Perform the rhodanese aggregation reaction* in the presence of Clusterin or purified Clu:DMPC nanodiscs** at molar ratio D-Rhod:Clu 1:3. * See protocol 'Prevention of rhodanese aggregation assay'. ** See 'Formation and isolation of Clu-phospholipid particles'. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bp2l6x59zlqe/v1

Centrifuge the rhodanese aggregation reactions in the presence of Clusterin or Clu:DMPC at 20000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes to remove big aggregates. NOTE : Here we use D-Rhodanese, but binding of other Clusterin substrates to Clusterin:DMPC nanodiscs can be tested and isolated using this assay.

Mix the supernatant with OptiPrep Density Gradient Medium (Merck, D1556, 60% iodixanol) to a final concentration of 36% iodixanol in PBS and final volume of 1 mL in a 3.5 mL Thickwall Polycarbonate Tube (349622, Beckman Coulter).
Carefully place 1 mL of 24% iodixanol diluted in PBS on top of the 36% iodixanol layer.
Carefully place 1 mL of PBS as top layer.
Centrifuge the gradient at 54000 rpm, 4 °C, 3 hours using a SW55 Ti rotor (Beckman Coulter).
After centrifugation, carefully collect 6 fractions of 0.5 mL .
Dilute each fraction with 0.5 mL PBS.
Add 40 µL of 2% Na-deoxycholate and vortex.
Incubate On ice for 00:15:00 .
Add 100 µL of 100% Trichloroacetic acid and vortex.
Incubate On ice for 01:00:00 .
Centrifuge the samples at 20000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .
Add 500 µL of ice-cold acetone.
Vortex or sonicate in a Bioruptor sonication bath (Diagenode) (2 cycles of 00:00:30 on – 00:00:30 off) for faster resuspension.
Centrifuge at 200000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes .
Air dry the pellets.
Add 30 µL 300 millimolar (mM) Tris pH 8.8 .
Incubate On ice for 00:05:00 .
Add 30 µL NuPAGE™ LDS Sample Buffer (4X) buffer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) containing 100 millimolar (mM) DTT and boil the samples for 00:10:00 .

Separate the proteins by sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE) followed by immunoblotting. NOTE : Isolated Clusterin phospholipid particles bound to a substrate can be also used for further experiments like cellular uptake, see Yeh FL et al. Neuron (2016).
creator: Patricia Yuste-Checa
